research_question: Can a portfolio-extraction-only challenger keep the v17 two-signal family and the v17 head-extraction geometry fixed, but add one deterministic cross-date target-weight stability cap that reduces incumbent resize flow enough to improve cost robustness without giving back the extraction gains?

score_contract_frozen.score_rule: mean(percentile_rank(momentum_60_5_raw DESC), percentile_rank(liquidity_trend_20_60_raw DESC)); require min_feature_count >= 2

guard_contract.guard_definition: Freeze the calibrated v7 / v17 ranking guard: only rows that are already ranking_eligible_D0 and whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional liquidity rank is >= 0.70 on the same signal_date may enter the ranking universe.
guard_contract.treatment_of_rows_below_guard: Remain in audit tables but must be excluded from ranking_state_daily eligibility.

portfolio_extraction_contract.extraction_definition: Freeze the v17 selected head and linear rank-decay geometry. On each signal_date, compute the same raw smoothed head weights as v17, then apply one deterministic absolute per-instrument daily target-weight change cap relative to the previous signal_date's mapped target weight before renormalizing selected-head weights to sum to 1.0.
portfolio_extraction_contract.weight_formula_detail: raw_multiplier(rank) = 1.00 - ((rank - 1) * (0.90 / 9)); rank 1 gets 1.00, rank 10 gets 0.10, ranks > 10 get 0.00

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same v17 score family and same calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard

motivation_from_v17_turnover_cost_decomp: The v17 turnover/cost decomposition showed that incumbent_resize_share_of_component_flow(存量调权换手占组件流量比) is about 0.80, so most turnover comes from resizing existing holdings rather than pure replacement flow.
The round exists to test whether one deterministic extraction stability cap can preserve the v17 extraction gains while directly reducing incumbent resize flow.
disallowed_changes: Do not change score features or score weights.
Do not change the calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard threshold or its stage.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not change sample labels or return definitions.
Do not sweep multiple stability-cap thresholds.
Do not sweep multiple extraction formulas.
Do not combine this round with refresh-rule, weight-penalty, or score-family changes.
notes: This round changes exactly one core dimension: portfolio extraction.
The purpose is to keep the productive v17 head geometry but suppress avoidable cross-date target-weight jumps that currently dominate turnover.
